description: Scenario catalogue for CLI-level workflows and observable outcomes.

scenarios.objective: Generate output files from a sample BibTeX input and a sample template.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
Output files are created in the target directory.
Generated content matches the expected rendered output for the fixture set.

scenarios.objective: Verify that dry-run mode reports intended outputs without writing files.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
The console output lists intended output file names and related entry keys.
No output files are created.

scenarios.objective: Verify overwrite and non-overwrite behaviour for existing output files.
expected_outcomes: With --overwrite, existing files are replaced without prompting.
Without --overwrite, existing files are not replaced and a warning is emitted for each skipped file.

scenarios.objective: Verify that supported file naming strategies produce the expected output names.
expected_outcomes: The uuid8 strategy generates stable, valid file names for the fixture expectations.
The slugify strategy generates valid file names derived from entry keys.

scenarios.objective: Verify that entries whose keys collide under a naming strategy produce disambiguated file names instead of silently overwriting one another within the same run.
expected_outcomes: Two entries whose keys slugify to the same stem produce distinct output files, the second suffixed with -2.
A warning identifying the colliding entry key is printed to stderr.
Neither entry's rendered content is lost or overwritten by the other.

scenarios.objective: Verify that declining the overwrite prompt for a pre-existing file does not mark that file name as reserved for same-run collision disambiguation.
expected_outcomes: Declining the overwrite prompt for the first colliding entry does not reserve its file name.
The second colliding entry targets the same pre-existing file name directly, without being disambiguated or misreported as a same-run collision.
Accepting the second entry's own overwrite prompt replaces the pre-existing file with that entry's rendered content.

scenarios.objective: Verify that single mode renders one output file for the selected entry set.
expected_outcomes: Exactly one output file is generated.
The output file contains rendered content for the selected entries.
The output file name is derived from the input file name and template file name.

scenarios.objective: Verify that transform supports entry-type filtering and rejects conflicting type selectors.
expected_outcomes: With --include-type, only entries of the selected types are rendered.
With --exclude-type, entries of excluded types are not rendered.
Supplying both --include-type and --exclude-type fails with an informative validation error.

scenarios.objective: Verify that a custom LaTeX substitution map overrides built-in substitutions for the latex_substitute helper.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully when the custom substitution map file is provided.
Rendered output applies custom substitutions in preference to built-in defaults.
Without the custom map option, rendered output uses the built-in default substitutions.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute applies custom substitutions with non-cascading semantics for replacement outputs.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully with a custom map where one replacement output resembles another key.
Rendered output keeps first-pass replacement output and does not apply chained substitutions in the same pass.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute preserves all supported math mode regions while still substituting matching LaTeX snippets outside those regions.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully for input containing mixed plain text and math-mode sections.
Rendered output substitutes matching LaTeX snippets outside math mode regions.
Rendered output leaves content inside $...$, $$...$$, \(...\), and \[...\] unchanged.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute preserves supported math mode regions when real built-in default-map tokens are present.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully for input containing real built-in LaTeX substitution tokens in mixed plain text and math-mode sections.
Rendered output applies built-in substitutions outside math mode regions.
Rendered output leaves real built-in tokens inside $...$, $$...$$, \(...\), and \[...\] unchanged.

scenarios.objective: Verify that latex_substitute treats an unclosed $$ opener as plain text while preserving a later valid $...$ math segment.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully for input containing an unclosed $$ opener before a later valid $...$ segment.
Rendered output treats the unmatched $$ opener as plain text and applies substitutions there.
Rendered output leaves the later well-formed $...$ segment unchanged.

scenarios.objective: Verify verbose logging and non-verbose progress behaviour.
expected_outcomes: Verbose mode emits per-entry diagnostic information and a summary.
Non-verbose mode emits progress-oriented output and a summary without detailed per-entry logging.

scenarios.command: transform with invalid input or template
scenarios.objective: Verify that transform fails immediately and reports informative errors for invalid artefacts.
expected_outcomes: The command exits with a failure status.
The reported error message identifies the relevant invalid or missing artefact.

scenarios.objective: Verify correct single-output rendering for a representative large real-world input file.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully for the large input fixture.
The generated single output is structurally correct for the selected template.
Processing completes without obvious failure modes associated with dataset size.

scenarios.objective: Verify the default information output when no input file is provided.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
The output describes the available fields for supported entry types.

scenarios.objective: Verify information output for entry types present in a supplied input file when no entry selection is made.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
The output describes the available fields for the entry types present in the input file.

scenarios.objective: Verify parsed key-value output for selected entries.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
The output contains parsed information only for the selected entries.

scenarios.objective: Verify parsed key-value output for selected entries when filtering by entry type.
expected_outcomes: With --include-type, output contains only selected entry types.
With --exclude-type, output excludes the specified entry types.
Supplying both --include-type and --exclude-type fails with an informative validation error.

scenarios.command: info and info --input without entry selection
scenarios.objective: Verify that the advertised entry-type field maps match the context actually exposed to Tera templates.
expected_outcomes: Neither the default map nor the input-derived map advertises fields.author, fields.title or fields.year.
The top-level authors, author_parts, title and year keys remain advertised for every entry type.
fields.month is only advertised for entry types that actually include a month field.

scenarios.objective: Verify that an explicit selection matching no entries produces an empty map and a warning instead of the unfiltered output.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully.
The standard output contains an empty JSON map.
A warning that the selection matched no entries is printed to standard error.

scenarios.objective: Verify that info verbose mode surfaces the underlying error cause chain.
expected_outcomes: The command exits with a failure status in both verbose and non-verbose modes.
Without --verbose, only the outermost error message is printed.
With --verbose, the error output includes the resolved configuration and the underlying cause chain.

scenarios.command: info --input with a representative selection on a large dataset
scenarios.objective: Verify representative parsed information output for a large real-world input file using a repeatable selection rule.
expected_outcomes: The command exits successfully for the large input fixture.
The output includes representative parsed key-value information for the selected entries in that file.
